column1: For client & team review · Priority: Core = launch-critical · Standard = full release · Enhanced = power features · Phase II = future

column5: Description
Users sign in with email and password to access the platform.
New users register with name, email, and password.
New users complete a guided 3-step setup: select role, choose interests, fill profile details.
Users log out from the profile menu and are returned to the login screen.
Users can switch between roles (Student, Professional, Company, Admin) from the profile menu. The platform adapts its content and navigation accordingly.
Top navigation with clearly labelled sections. Active section is visually highlighted.
On mobile, a hamburger menu opens a full navigation drawer. A fixed bottom bar provides one-tap access to key sections.
Nav items adapt per role — Company users see 'My Listings'; Admin users see 'Admin Dashboard'.
Click profile avatar to access role switcher, settings, messages, and logout.
column8: For mobile view - profile, settings is not found

column5: Header bell icon shows unread count. Clicking opens a dropdown listing recent notifications (applications received, reviews, connection requests, system alerts). Notifications can be marked as read.
Pending connection requests are shown in the header and can be accepted or rejected in one click.
column8: For mobile view, connection request is pending

column5: Homepage greets the user by name with role-appropriate context and content.
First-time users see actionable setup steps: complete profile, explore jobs, connect with peers.
Horizontally scrollable row of recommended jobs and projects based on the user's profile.
Displays the user's active mentorships, supervisions, projects, and event registrations with quick navigation links.
Cards showing the user's next events with date and location at a glance.
Suggested mentors and courses personalised to the user's interests.
Students see internship and early-career content; Professionals see consulting opportunities; Companies see a hiring dashboard shortcut.
Scrollable job listings with a filter sidebar to narrow by location, job type, experience level, salary range, and work mode.
Each listing shows title, company, location, salary, job type, and a save toggle. Featured jobs are visually highlighted.
Full listing view: description, requirements, compensation, benefits, company info, and apply CTA.
Students automatically see only internships, volunteering roles, and early-career jobs — other listings are filtered out.
Users can save jobs and view them in a dedicated 'Saved' tab.
The detail page surfaces a row of similar jobs to encourage discovery.
When results are sparse, users are prompted to explore related modules like Projects or Events.
One-click application using the user's platform profile. User reviews a profile snapshot, optionally adds a cover letter, and submits.
For jobs with an external application portal, users are directed to the employer's site.
Users track all job applications in one place with status labels: Applied, Shortlisted, Accepted, Hired, Rejected.
Share any job listing via WhatsApp, email, LinkedIn, or copy link.
Users can flag a job as inappropriate with a reason. Reports are sent to the admin queue.
Companies create a job through a guided 4-step form: Basic Info → Requirements & Skills → Compensation & Benefits → Review.
A job can be saved as a draft at any step and returned to later.
Before submission, the poster sees exactly how the job will appear to applicants.
A mandatory pre-submission checklist ensures the listing meets platform standards: non-discriminatory language, salary disclosed, valid deadline, contact details.
On submission, the listing enters a review queue. It goes live only after admin approval.
Posters can edit any draft or active listing using the same guided form.
Company sees all their job listings organised by status: Drafts, Under Review, Active, Rejected, Closed.
Click any listing to see all applicants with their profile, application date, and current status.
Update each applicant's status: Pending → Shortlisted → Accepted → Hired or Rejected.
Each listing card has a menu to Edit, Close, Extend deadline, Delete, Duplicate, or Archive.
Admin sees all submitted jobs awaiting review, filterable by status.
Admin can approve (listing goes live), reject (with a message to the poster), or request edits.
Admin can select multiple listings and approve them in one action.
Scrollable project listings with filters for type, segment, format, level, and duration.
Cards show title, organisation, duration, and required skills. Detail page includes team roles, compensation, and timeline.
Users can save projects and share them via link, WhatsApp, or email.
Applicants submit a short Statement of Interest alongside their profile when applying for a project.
4-step guided form: Basics → Team & Details → Compensation & Timeline → Review. Both Companies and Professionals can post.
Same draft-save, compliance checklist, and admin-approval flow as Jobs.
All project listings organised by status with applicant counts.
View applicant profiles and update their status.
Admin review queue for submitted project listings.
Browse referral opportunities sorted by urgency and deadline, with urgency badges on cards.
Cards show specialization, location, urgency, and deadline. Detail page includes prerequisites, client info, and experience requirements.
Save referrals and share via link or messaging apps.
Users submit a note and qualifications to express interest in a referral opportunity.
3-step form: Basic Info → Details & Requirements → Review. Both Companies and Professionals can create referrals.
6-item compliance checklist covering confidentiality, ethics, accuracy, and deadlines before submission.
All referral listings organised by status (Draft, Pending, Active, Closed).
See all who responded and update their status: Pending → Shortlisted → Accepted / Declined.
Admin review queue for submitted referral listings.
Course listings with filters for specialization, mode (online/offline), duration, fees, and outcome.
Cards show title, provider, mode, duration, and rating. Detail page includes full curriculum, schedule, instructor profile, and prerequisites.
Each course shows a star rating with a full review section: rating distribution, individual reviews, and a write-a-review form.
Save courses for later and share via link or messaging apps.
Course sessions can be saved directly to the user's calendar via a downloadable .ics file.
Enrolment checks prerequisites from the user's profile, confirms details, and submits. A success confirmation is shown.
Users see all courses in one place with tabs for Explore, Enrolled, Saved, and Completed.
Companies can nominate employees for a course in bulk by selecting from their team roster.
Companies can submit a training request describing their team's learning need.
Multi-step form: Course Info → Curriculum & Schedule → Pricing & Enrolment → Preview. Both Companies and Professionals can post.
Same draft-save, compliance checklist, and admin-approval flow.
All course listings with enrolment counts and status badges.
Review each enrolment request and update status: Accepted, Waitlisted, Rejected, or Enrolled.
Admin review queue for submitted course listings.
Event listings with filters for status, type, format, and location. Cards display tags like Trending, Featured, and Upcoming.
Cards show title, date, location, and event type. Detail page includes agenda, speakers, venue, and all participation options.
Save events, share via link or apps, or flag inappropriate listings.
Any event can be saved to the user's calendar via a downloadable .ics file.
Users register their attendance through a quick RSVP form.
Users submit an abstract and upload a paper for events with a Call for Papers.
Users submit a speaker bio and proposed topics for events seeking speakers.
Organisations can submit interest in sponsoring an event by selecting a tier and providing details.
Extended guided form: Basic Info → Schedule & Venue → Speakers & Agenda → Participation Options (toggle RSVP, Papers, Speakers, Sponsorship) → Preview.
Organisers choose which participation types to enable for each event and configure each form individually.
Same draft-save, compliance checklist, and admin-approval flow.
All event listings with registration counts per participation type.
Separate tabs for RSVPs, paper submissions, speaker applications, and sponsorship interests. Each registrant's status can be updated.
Admin review queue for submitted event listings.
Supervision listings with filter controls. Supervisor cards show specialization, mode, star rating, and fee.
Full profile: bio, domain, specializations, session mode, fee, and reviews with a direct apply CTA.
Save supervisors and share listings via link or apps.
Users apply directly from the supervisor's profile through a confirmation modal.
Multi-step guided form with brochure upload. Both Companies and Professionals can post.
Same draft-save, compliance checklist, and admin-approval flow.
All supervision listings organised by status with applicant counts.
Review applicants and update status: Pending → Accepted / Waitlisted / Rejected / Enrolled.
After acceptance, both supervisor and supervisee access a dedicated workspace with all collaboration tools in one place.
Schedule supervision sessions with date, time, and agenda. Sessions appear in a calendar view.
View all participants with their roles, status, and contact options.
Threaded discussion board for questions and replies within the supervision group.
Upload or link resources that all participants can download or view.
Log sessions, mark attendance, and track cumulative hours for each participant.
Review performance metrics and add notes on each participant's progress.
Display the supervision agreement, platform rules, and expectations for all participants.
Sessions can be saved to personal calendars via downloadable .ics files.
Admin review queue for submitted supervision listings.
Mentoring cohort listings with filter controls. Mentor cards show specialization, cohort type, star rating, and fee.
Full profile: bio, domain, mentorship focus, fee, and reviews with a direct join CTA.
Save mentor listings and share via link or apps.
Users apply to join a cohort from the mentor's profile through a confirmation modal.
Multi-step guided form for creating a mentoring cohort. Both Companies and Professionals can post.
Same draft-save, compliance checklist, and admin-approval flow.
All cohort listings organised by status with applicant counts.
Review applicants and update status: Pending → Accepted / Waitlisted / Rejected / Enrolled.
Accepted cohort members access a shared workspace with all collaboration tools.
Schedule both 1:1 and group sessions with calendar display.
View all cohort members with their roles.
Threaded Q&A board for cohort questions and discussions.
Upload or link learning resources for all cohort members.
Log sessions and track cumulative hours for each cohort member.
Track performance and add progress notes for each member.
Sessions can be saved to personal calendars via downloadable .ics files.
Admin review queue for submitted mentoring cohort listings.
Funding listings with filters for type, eligibility, amount range, and deadline.
Cards show funder, amount, deadline, and eligibility. Detail page covers description, application process, and full eligibility criteria.
Browse community circles by topic or interest and join to access their feed.
Inside a circle, members see posts with likes, comments, and shares in a scrollable feed.
Members create posts with text and optional images within any circle they've joined.
Users can start a new community circle with a name, description, category, and privacy setting.
Peer pods are small groups for peer support. Users can browse by topic and join pods of interest.
An active session view showing participants, agenda, timer, notes, and a discussion space.
Before joining a pod, users must accept a confidentiality agreement to protect session privacy.
Users can create a new peer pod with a topic, size limit, schedule, and description.
A public forum where members share thoughts, insights, and experiences in a post feed.
Full post view with a comments thread and reactions.
Users submit a titled post with content and tags for the broader community to read.
Search and browse platform members with filters. Cards show name, role, specialization, and location.
Full profile page accessible from any person card.
Send connection requests and message any member directly from their card or profile.
Search and browse companies with filters. Cards show name, industry, size, and location.
Full company page: overview, active listings, team members, company updates, and a follow button.
The Network section also surfaces event listings, giving users another path to discover events.
Personal profile with tabs: About (bio, education, experience, skills, certifications), Activity, Connections, Settings, and Reviews.
A visible role badge (Student, Professional, Company, Admin) appears on the user's profile and cards throughout the platform.
Others can leave reviews and star ratings on a user's profile. The tab shows a rating summary and all reviews received.
Users control which types of notifications they receive by email, toggled per category (Jobs, Events, Courses, etc.).
A dedicated profile for mentors showing bio, domain, mentorship focus, fee, reviews, and a request mentorship CTA.
A dedicated profile for supervisors showing bio, domain, session mode, fee, and reviews.
A dedicated profile for companies showing overview, active listings, team, and recent updates.
A central hub for companies showing total listings, active listings, total applications, and listings pending review — all in one stats row.
Tabs across the dashboard for each module: Jobs, Projects, Courses, Events, Supervision, Mentoring, Referrals.
Within each module tab, filter listings by All, Drafts, Under Review, Active, Rejected, Changes Needed, or Closed.
Each listing card has a menu to Edit, Close, Extend deadline, Delete, Duplicate, or Archive.
A visual progress timeline on each listing card showing where it is in its lifecycle.
Change a listing's status directly from the dashboard without opening the full edit form.
Search, filter, and save profiles of platform members. Download CVs (consent-gated) and send connection requests.
Submit a training request describing learning needs, team size, and budget for course providers to respond to.
Submit a supervision request for a team or individual.
Submit a mentoring request for a team or cohort.
A summary view showing pending review counts per module, recent platform activity, and flagged items.
All submitted listings across every module appear in a unified review queue, organised by module tabs and filterable by status.
Admin approves a listing; it immediately goes live on the platform.
Admin rejects a listing with a written message. The poster is notified and can revise and resubmit.
Admin can request specific changes from the poster without fully rejecting the listing.
Select multiple listings and approve them in a single action.
All user-flagged listings appear here with the reporter's identity, reason, and the listing in context.
Admin can warn the poster, remove the listing, or dismiss the report.
Charts showing: listings created per month, applications per listing, time-to-approve, listings by module, and user growth over time.
Add, edit, or delete the platform's categories, specializations, skills, and filter options used across all modules.
Users can message any other member from their profile or card. A full-screen conversation view shows message history with sent/received bubbles.
Search through existing conversations by name to quickly find a thread.
After completing a course or supervision, users leave a star rating and written review. Reviews display with a rating distribution bar.
Users can receive reviews on their profile, visible to anyone who visits it.
Every listing across all modules can be shared via WhatsApp, email, LinkedIn, or a copy link.
Any user can flag a listing as inappropriate. Reports go directly to the admin reports queue.
Fees, salaries, and compensation across the platform display in the correct currency symbol (₹ INR, $ USD, £ GBP, € EUR).
